meta.name: SWEMWBS Short Warwick-Edinburgh Mental Well-Being Scale
meta.description: The SWEMWBS is a 7-item scale derived from the full 14-item WEMWBS, measuring mental well-being with a focus on functioning rather than feeling. It provides a single score reflecting overall mental well-being over the past two weeks.
meta.author: Stewart-Brown, Tennant, Tennant, Platt, Parkinson & Weich (2009)
meta.reference: Stewart-Brown S, Tennant A, Tennant R, Platt S, Parkinson J, Weich S. Internal construct validity of the Warwick-Edinburgh Mental Well-Being Scale (WEMWBS): a Rasch analysis using data from the Scottish Health Education Population Survey. Health Qual Life Outcomes. 2009;7:15.
meta.instruction: Below are some statements about feelings and thoughts. Please select the option that best describes your experience of each over the last 2 weeks.

questions.text: I've been feeling optimistic about the future
I've been dealing with problems well
I've been feeling close to other people
I've been able to make up my own mind about things

ranges.label: Low Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is significantly below average, indicating limited positive functioning, low optimism, and reduced engagement with life. This level of low well-being is associated with elevated risk for depression and anxiety, and professional support is recommended.
suggestions: Seek support from a mental health professional to address persistent low well-being
Start with small daily activities that bring a sense of achievement or pleasure
Connect with supportive people — social relationships are key to well-being recovery
Establish regular sleep, exercise, and nutrition routines as a foundation for improvement

ranges.label: Below Average Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is below average, suggesting reduced positive feelings and functioning in daily life. You may experience lower confidence, less engagement with others, or diminished optimism. Targeted strategies can help improve your well-being over time.
suggestions: Identify specific areas of well-being that feel most lacking and focus efforts there
Increase engagement in meaningful activities such as hobbies, volunteering, or learning
Strengthen social connections by reaching out to friends, family, or community groups
Practice positive psychology exercises such as gratitude journaling or strengths identification
Consider speaking with a counselor if low well-being persists

ranges.label: Above Average Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is above average, reflecting generally positive feelings and good functioning. You likely experience reasonable optimism, confidence, and engagement with the people and activities around you. Continue nurturing the habits that support your well-being.
suggestions: Maintain the relationships and activities that contribute to your positive well-being
Explore new interests or challenges to sustain growth and engagement
Practice mindfulness or reflection to deepen your appreciation of positive experiences
Support others around you — helping others also strengthens personal well-being

ranges.label: High Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is high, indicating strong positive feelings, optimism, confidence, and active engagement with life. This level of well-being is a significant protective factor against mental health difficulties and supports resilience during challenging times.
suggestions: Continue the practices and relationships that sustain your high well-being
Use your positive resources to take on new challenges and support others
Stay mindful that well-being can fluctuate — maintain healthy habits even during good times
